chapters.title: Foundations of AI Engineering
chapters.goal: Set up a professional AI dev environment, call LLM APIs confidently, and write prompts that produce reliable, structured outputs.

items.title: Setting up your AI engineering environment
Your first LLM API call — how OpenAI works
Conversations and chat history

Prompt engineering fundamentals

Multi-model AI assistant with structured output

chapters.title: AI System Design
chapters.goal: Design LLM-based systems end-to-end and implement any pattern — with or without a framework.

items.title: What can you build with LLMs? — system types and the Staircase
Cognitive architecture — designing data flow
The golden rule: use as little AI as possible
LLM call chaining and parallelization
Tool calling — giving your LLM hands
Agentic loops — letting the LLM decide what to do next
Evaluator-optimizer and LLM routers
Build a multi-step document processing pipeline
LangChain and LangGraph — orchestration explained
PydanticAI — type-safe AI workflows
Building agents from scratch — no framework needed
Build a research agent from scratch
Automated research assistant

chapters.title: Production AI Backends
chapters.goal: Build a scalable backend that exposes AI capabilities via a clean API, runs in Docker, and persists data.

chapters.title: RAG & Vector Search
chapters.goal: Build a production RAG pipeline that connects an LLM to custom data, with measurable retrieval quality.

chapters.title: Monitoring & Evaluation
chapters.goal: Measure, track, and improve the quality, cost, and reliability of a live AI system.

chapters.title: Deployment & Production
chapters.goal: Deploy, monitor, and maintain an AI application in a real production environment.
